question: Does the generated image include the specific woman from the reference, preserving her facial features and long brown hair?
Is the woman wearing the red satin slip dress from the reference image?
Is the Shiba Inu from the reference image present, preserving its orange/tan coat and curled tail?
Is the blue-and-white porcelain vase from the reference image present, preserving its floral patterns and shape?
Is the museum hall image used as the background, featuring white walls, columns, and a polished black marble floor?
Is the woman positioned in the right-center midground of the image?
Is the woman's body facing left while her gaze is directed at the camera?
Is the Shiba Inu positioned in the lower-left foreground of the image?
Is the Shiba Inu scaled appropriately for the foreground, appearing as a real-sized dog rather than a toy?
Is the porcelain vase placed in the background near the right-side column?
Is the vase partially occluded by the right-side column to create realistic depth?
Do all three subjects (woman, dog, vase) have visible reflections on the polished black marble floor?
Do the floor reflections fade in intensity and become slightly blurred with distance?
Are there distinct, thin contact shadows at the feet/paws/base of the subjects to prevent a floating appearance?
Have the two visitors from the original background image been completely removed?
Are there visible smudging artifacts or distortions in the area where the background visitors were removed?
Does the lighting on the subjects match the cool overhead skylight ambience of the museum hall?
Are the highlights on the red dress toned down to prevent clipping or overexposure?
Is a subtle film grain applied to the image to simulate an authentic photograph?
Does the image convey a wide-angle photographic perspective consistent with the museum hall reference?
Are there any severe anatomical distortions in the subjects (e.g., extra limbs, distorted faces)?
Are there visible compositing artifacts such as halos or jagged edges around the subjects?
